pattern: limit depends on directions, not initial triangle
bridge_to_triangle_limit.source_model: Limit construction for triangles extended along fixed directions
thalean_translation: local t -> local action extension parameter
limiting triangle -> normalized local-I register shape

definition_stack.definition_candidate: A local receipt center or forward socket inside a bounded candidate register.
definition_stack.name: local I

definition_stack.definition_candidate: The local extension/action parameter measuring how far a boundary is extended along an admitted direction.
definition_stack.name: local t

definition_stack.definition_candidate: A direction or socket constraint that remains stable while local candidates extend by t.
definition_stack.name: fixed direction

definition_stack.definition_candidate: A shared normalization center over the G15 quotient used to compare local I centers.
definition_stack.name: G15 centroid

definition_stack.definition_candidate: A local I expressed relative to the G15 centroid.
definition_stack.name: normalized I

definition_stack.definition_candidate: One of observed_receipted, proxy_invalid, or hard_absence_gate_absent in the slot/C superposition register.
definition_stack.name: receipt status

keeper_line: A theorem begins when local t, local I, and the centroid all agree on the same register.

next_work.purpose: Make G15 centroid precise enough for theorem work.
Define the local I points and their coordinates before normalization.
Run a finite analogue of local t extension and centroid normalization.
Decide which theorem claims can be promoted to theorem candidates.

theorem_sequence.claim_candidate: Local extension by t along fixed directions admits centroid normalization.
proof_inputs_needed: define finite affine embedding or quotient coordinates

theorem_sequence.claim_candidate: After centroid normalization and scaling, the limiting register shape depends on direction/socket data rather than initial local body placement.
proof_inputs_needed: bounded initial term versus t-scaled direction term

theorem_sequence.claim_candidate: Local I centers become comparable after normalization through the G15 centroid.
proof_inputs_needed: invariance or equivariance check under allowed relabeling

theorem_sequence.claim_candidate: The slot/C register partitions into observed_receipted, proxy_invalid, and hard_absence_gate_absent under the admitted gate candidates.
